title: G2: Impropriety lemma — orbit-constant $\pi$ is non-normalizable

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: Prove rigorously: if $\pi\ge0$ satisfies $\pi(x)=\pi(x/2)$ for (Lebesgue-)a.e. $x>0$, then either $\pi\equiv0$ or $\int_0^\infty \pi(x)\,dx=\infty$. Hence the naive $\tfrac54 x$ computation presupposes an **improper** prior. Consequences to prove: under such a prior, $E[X]$ and $E[Y]$ are undefined, the marginal of $X$ is not a probability measure, and the unconditional symmetry argument ($E[Y]=E[X]$) cannot be written. Address both the discrete (mass on a doubling orbit $\{x_0 2^n\}$) and continuous formulations, with measure-theoretic care (a.e., $\sigma$-finiteness). Cite Dawid–Stone–Zidek (1973) marginalization paradoxes for the inconsistency of conditioning under improper priors.
